exclude.G204: All subprocess launches use argv form (exec.Command/CommandContext with separate arguments, never 'sh -c'). User-controlled build inputs (package atom, USE flags, versions, env keys/values) are validated against strict allowlists in internal/builder/validate.go before reaching any command, and container execution passes emerge as argv with env via 'docker exec -e', so no value is shell-interpreted.
exclude.G304: File paths passed to os.Open/os.ReadFile come from operator configuration (config files, GNUPGHOME, PKGDIR, terraform workspace) or from server-generated bundle paths, not from unauthenticated request bodies. Individual call sites carry inline #nosec G304 justifications.
exclude.G124: Flagged cookies are either outbound request cookies to the PVE API (PVEAuthCookie — HttpOnly/Secure are response-cookie attributes and do not apply) or the dashboard session cookie, which already sets HttpOnly + SameSite=Lax; Secure depends on the TLS deployment and cannot be forced for internal HTTP.
exclude.G703: Experimental taint-analysis rule (path traversal). The flagged artifact-download paths are validated against the job's recorded artifact list (GetArtifactPath/GetArtifactPathByRel) and never install to the host; they are not attacker-controlled filesystem paths.
exclude.G704: Experimental taint-analysis rule (SSRF). The flagged fetches are fixed reverse proxies to the operator-configured server/builder URL (config, not request bodies) — the same rationale as G304 for file paths.
